mnemonics.title: MOSPF Key Features
mnemonics.content: MOSPF = Multicast + OSPF Extension - Uses Group Membership LSAs to build shortest path trees for each source-group pair

mnemonics.title: Multicast Modes
mnemonics.content: Dense Mode = Flood then Prune | Sparse Mode = Join then Forward - Think Dense = Dense receivers, Sparse = Spread out receivers

mnemonics.title: RPB Process
mnemonics.content: RPF Check: Is this packet from the direction of source? YES = Forward, NO = Drop

mnemonics.title: MBONE Components
mnemonics.content: MRs (Multicast Routers) + Tunnels = Virtual Multicast Network over Unicast Infrastructure

acronyms.usage: Multicast routing protocol extending OSPF for group membership
Protocol for hosts to join/leave multicast groups
First multicast protocol used in MBONE
Technique to prevent loops in multicast routing
Multicast protocol using shared tree approach
Check to verify correct incoming interface for multicast
Root of shared tree in sparse mode protocols
Multicast where receivers specify the source

visualTips.visualization: Draw one source (S) connected to three receivers (R1,R2,R3). Unicast = 3 separate lines from S to each R. Multicast = 1 line from S branching to all Rs like a tree.
Show source at center, draw packet radiating outward. Only forward if received on interface toward source - draw X on wrong-direction packets.
Shared Tree: Central circle (RP) with lines to all nodes. Source Tree: Direct lines from source (S) to each receiver (R1,R2,R3) like spokes.

visualTips.visualize: Draw two islands (multicast-capable networks) separated by ocean (non-multicast network). Draw tunnel bridge connecting them.

analogies.analogy: Like a radio station broadcasting to all tuned-in receivers - one transmission reaches many listeners.
Like a river branching into delta - water (data) flows from source and splits at junctions to reach all destinations.
Like shouting in a room - everyone hears initially, then those not interested say 'stop' (prune).
Like subscribing to a magazine - you explicitly request to join (receive), not added by default.
Like all flights going through one hub airport - efficient for routing but creates congestion at the hub (RP).
